turns.label: Day 1
turns.briefing: Morning intake: verify customs data against the newspaper bulletin and rulebook.

documents.title: Inspection Rulebook (Excerpt)
documents.content:
Duty rates:
- Electronics: 12%
- Textiles: 8%
- Medical: 2%

Secondary check triggers:
1) Missing batch code
2) Country mismatch
3) Weight gap over 10%

Gate handling:
Gate C handles perishable and medical cargo.

documents.title: Border Times - Daily Bulletin
documents.headline: Battery Fraud Surge Triggers Border Crackdown
documents.subhead: Customs officers ordered to tighten classification checks for electronics shipments.
columns: The customs bureau issued a fresh warning after multiple battery consignments were found mislabeled as low-risk parts. Inspection teams are now required to cross-check declared duty class against manifest item detail before release.

Supervisors said routing mistakes remain a secondary risk. Officers must confirm lane and gate assignments against yard maps before stamping approvals. Violations are expected to trigger immediate escalation and temporary holding procedures.

documents.title: Yard Gate Map
documents.imagePrompt: Top-down yard map graphic with clearly labeled Gate A, Gate B, Gate C and lane markers. Gate C should be visually highlighted as medical/perishable.

questions.prompt: Electronics cargo uses a duty rate of ___%.
Which gate should receive medical cargo?
In 3-4 sentences, explain why electronics now require stricter checks.
questions.idealAnswer: Electronics are under increased scrutiny due to mislabeling incidents. Officers should verify class, duty rate, and route to reduce fraud and safety risk.

turns.label: Day 2
turns.briefing: Cross-check warehouse spreadsheet and warning memo before approving release.

documents.title: Supervisor Memo
documents.content:
Escalation rule:
Any shipment with a weight gap above 10% must be flagged.

Temporary lane closure:
Gate B closed 14:00-18:00 for maintenance.

questions.prompt: Which shipment should be escalated first based on the largest gap?
Select all entries that violate the 10% gap rule.
Write a short release note (50-70 words) instructing drivers how to proceed while Gate B is closed.
questions.idealAnswer: Reference closure window, rerouting to active gates, and requirement to keep manifests ready for re-check.

turns.label: Day 3
turns.briefing: Resolve labeling anomalies with the photo sheet and witness transcript.

documents.title: Container Label Photo
documents.imagePrompt: Photo-style evidence board showing a container label 'TX', a green lane sticker, and a visual note that green corresponds to medical lane only.

documents.title: Witness Transcript
entries: Driver: I followed the green sticker to Lane 1.
Inspector: Green is medical only today.
Driver: My manifest is marked textile, code TX.
Inspector: Then lane assignment is inconsistent.

questions.prompt: Pair each cargo code with the correct lane.
The transcript indicates TX cargo was correctly routed.
Write two follow-up questions you would ask the driver.
questions.idealAnswer: Questions should ask route decision source and who attached sticker tags.

turns.label: Day 4
turns.briefing: Timed competency day: process order, revision cards, and term precision.

documents.title: Vocabulary Sheet
documents.content:
Discrepancy:
Mismatch between declared and observed values.

Escalation:
Transfer to secondary review due to risk triggers.

documents.title: Flashcard Prompt
documents.content:
Front:
When do you escalate a shipment?

Back:
On policy triggers (mismatch, missing code, lane conflict, severe weight gap).

questions.prompt: Type the back-side response from memory.
questions.idealAnswer: Escalate on mismatch, missing code, lane conflict, or large weight gap.

questions.expected: mismatch between declared and observed values
questions.idealAnswer: A discrepancy is a mismatch between declared and observed values.

turns.label: Day 5
turns.briefing: Final practical: submit evidence and written justification.

documents.title: Final Dossier Bulletin
documents.headline: Lane Mismatch Case Referred for Final Decision
documents.subhead: Electronics shipment found in textile lane during random audit.
columns: Inspectors reported a conflict between manifest class and physical route assignment. The cargo record stated electronics while lane stickers and dock logs reflected textile handling.
Policy guidance states that such mismatches require immediate hold status, written evidence capture, and escalation to secondary review before any release can be considered.

documents.title: Final Evidence Image
documents.imagePrompt: Evidence-style photo showing electronics manifest details near textile lane signage to indicate route mismatch. Keep text labels readable.

documents.title: Submission Guide
documents.content:
Required deliverables:
1) Written summary document
2) Clear evidence reference
3) Recommended action with reason

questions.prompt: Highlight the key evidence image and drag that image into this answer area.
questions.idealAnswer: The dropped image should be the evidence photo with meaningful highlights on route/class mismatch details.

questions.prompt: Write a final recommendation memo (80-120 words).
questions.idealAnswer: Memo should recommend hold + escalation and cite manifest/lane mismatch evidence.

questions.prompt: Draft two supervisor questions to close this case safely.
questions.idealAnswer: Questions should verify source of lane assignment and confirm who validated manifest class.
